action.reasoning: Observation: current frozen CLIP-B + linear head pipeline is working end-to-end, local prediction works, and official eval is 0.2339 macro-F1. Training is now fast enough (~6 min for feature extraction + head fit), so we have room to improve. Analysis: the main weakness is likely underfitting/domain shift from a single global linear head on center-cropped features. A strong low-cost upgrade is to use CLIP ViT-L/14 frozen features, multi-view feature extraction (center + flip) for train/val, and a stronger regularized MLP head trained on cached features with class-balanced loss. This should improve representation quality without the huge cost of end-to-end finetuning. Decision: patch model/train/predict to support cached frozen CLIP features, optional ViT-L backbone, 2-view feature averaging, and an MLP classifier with consistent checkpointing; train a short baseline with ViT-L and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --model_name openai/clip-vit-large-patch14 --epochs 12 --batch_size 96 --head_batch_size 2048 --lr 2e-3 --weight_decay 5e-4 --dropout 0.3 --hidden_dim 1024 --use_flip --save_path checkpoints/best.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_preds.json
